summary: Set 027 Session 3: @vscode/test-electron tree-provider harness. Built Python CLI shim (harness_cli.py, 305 LOC) wrapping Session 1/2 fixture helpers + TS harness module (e2eHarness.ts, 365 LOC) that drives it via subprocess. Shipped five TS test files exercising SessionSetsProvider.getChildren(): treeProvider-happy (6 tests), -cancel (4 tests), -force (3 tests), -multiset (4 tests after Round C added in-progress sort coverage), -worktree (3 tests). 20 new e2e tests + 306 existing unit tests pass via `npm run test:unit` (326 total, 2 pre-existing failures unrelated). Discovered + pinned two real drift classes the harness exists to catch: (1) register_session_start omits empty completedSessions[], defeating Set 022's in-flight predicate on fresh sets; (2) isMidSetComplete downgrades mid-set complete snapshots so a force-closed mid-set surfaces in In Progress with [FORCED], not Done. Neither bug fixed in this session — pinned as current behavior with explicit test comments. Cross-provider verification ($0.597, gpt-5-4, 3 rounds, ~1500 LOC) surfaced 5 Major + 4 Minor findings; all addressed. npm test (full test-electron) blocked by pre-existing Code.exe 1.120 / test-electron 2.5.2 launch-arg incompatibility on this Windows host — environmental, not session-introduced; documented for follow-up.

reason.specifics: Session 4 adds the Playwright Electron rendering smoke layer + closes the set. Same TS / Electron territory as Session 3, and the harness's Python fixture generator + TS shape helpers are already proven on this orchestrator. Operator should also re-evaluate whether the Layer 3 Playwright smoke is still warranted now that Layer 2 catches text-content drift; the spec's open question #1 explicitly allows trimming Layer 3 scope at Session 4 start.
